body:0002.text: The Management Feed helps admins monitor recent actions on the platform for greater transparency and accountability. It is located in the Dashboard tab of your Admin Panel.

body:0003.text: Only admins can access this tab, it is not available to project managers or normal users.

body:0004.text: ⚠️ If you do not see this tab, your current price plan may not include it.

body:0005.text: Which actions are displayed in the management feed?

body:0006.text: The management feed displays the following action types:

body:0007.text: Created: when a new input, phase, project, or folder is added.

body:0008.text: Modified: when an existing element is changed.

body:0010.text: Some “Modified” entries include a View details link. Clicking it reveals the exact changes made. If the link is missing, the action was a publication (no detailed change log).

body:0012.text: An entry might show that John Doe edited the start and end dates of the phase “Mapping Urban Planning: Demo” in the project “Survey Mapping Toolbox.”

body:0015.text: Keys ending in _multiloc indicate a text field (e.g., survey titles or buttons).

body:0016.text: How to monitor and filter activity?

body:0017.text: Open the Dashboard tab in your Admin Panel.

body:0019.text: Use the filters at the top of the feed to refine results:

body:0020.text: Filter by Project: View all actions on a specific project within the last 30 days.

body:0021.text: Filter by User: View all actions performed by a specific admin or project manager within the last 30 days.

body:0022.text: Review the filtered results to identify trends, monitor workloads, or target training needs.

body:0023.text: Where can you monitor user activity?

body:0024.text: In the Users tab of your Admin Panel, the Last Active column shows when each person last took an action on the platform.

body:0028.text: Spot areas where re-engagement might be needed
